nodes.context: Criminal gang based in Tabasco, Mexico, involved in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, murder, kidnapping, arms trafficking, and illegal fuel extraction.
Mexican drug cartel that operated in Acapulco; split after leader Arturo Beltrán Leyva was killed in 2009.
Criminal gang formed from a split of the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel; rival of La Barredora.
Mexican drug cartel; La Barredora has links with it.
Faction of Los Zetas in Central Mexico; ally of La Barredora.

edges.evidence_quote: The criminal group came into existence during the rapid decentralization of Mexico's drug trafficking organizations and as a split-off group of the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.
edges.context: La Barredora split off from the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The cartel is known to battle its rival, the Independent Cartel of Acapulco
edges.context: La Barredora battles the Independent Cartel of Acapulco as rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: to have links with the Sinaloa Cartel
edges.context: La Barredora has links with the Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Los Talibanes (Faction of Los Zetas in Central Mexico)
edges.context: Los Talibanes are allies of La Barredora.

edges.evidence_quote: the Independent Cartel of Acapulco (also an offshoot of the Beltrán Leyva Cartel)
edges.context: The Independent Cartel of Acapulco is an offshoot of the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.
